nodes.title: Problem understanding
nodes.summary: Captures what is given, what must be produced, and the main input/output goal.
nodes.evidence_text: Given $$$p$$$ and $$$q$$$, determine whether there exist positive integers $$$n$$$ and $$$m$$$ such that an $$$n \times m$$$ grid can be formed using exactly $$$p$$$ unit-length segments and $$$q$$$ L-shaped pieces, possibly rotating them, and provide any valid $$$n$$$ and $$$m$$$.

nodes.title: Mathematical model
nodes.summary: Represents the mathematical condition or formula behind the solution.
nodes.evidence_text: $$$p+2q = m(n+1) + n(m+1)$$$ is quadratic on $$$m$$$ and $$$n$$$.

nodes.summary: Core observation used to reduce the problem.

nodes.evidence_text: $$$ So we can iterate $$$n$$$ from $$$1$$$ to $$$\left\lfloor\sqrt{p/2+q}\right\rfloor$$$ and, for each $$$n$$$, check if $$$ m = \frac{p+2q-n}{2n+1} $$$ is a positive integer such that $$$\vert m-n\vert \le p$$$.
